title: xiaoyi-ssg Source Definition Schema
description: A single data source definition. Every source is resolved by a Source Adapter into a normalized array of items. New source kinds are added by writing a new adapter under .xiaoyi-ssg/sources/ and adding a `type` here; the engine main loop does not change. Full contract, security, cache and fallback rules: prompts/data-sources.md.

type.description: Adapter kind. `markdown` = local source/<name>/*.md (default content). `http` = build-time fetch of a remote API. `json`/`csv` = local or remote structured file. `rss` = feed. `inline` = literal items embedded in the manifest. `derived` = computed from another source.

sort.description: Applied after the adapter produces items and after any map/normalize step.

tree.description: Build a tree structure from `parent`/`nav_order` fields (for sidebar navigation). Applies to any source kind, not just markdown.

map.description: Field remapping to normalize adapter output into the standard item shape. Keys are target fields (title, slug, date, cover, excerpt, tags, ...); values are source field names or JSONPath-lite expressions. Fields not listed are still carried through onto the item.

defaults.description: Default values merged under each item when a mapped/normalized field is absent (e.g. a `date` for API items that lack one).

dir.description: type=markdown: content directory relative to site root, e.g. 'source/posts'. User-managed source subdirectories must use conventional names without a leading underscore.

url.description: type=http|json|csv|rss: absolute URL fetched at BUILD TIME only. Never fetched in the browser. Never a source of secrets in itself.

headers.description: type=http: static request headers (non-secret). For secrets use `auth`.

body.description: type=http POST: request body. Object is JSON-encoded; string is sent as-is.

auth.description: Secret injection. The secret value is read from process.env at build time and MUST NOT be written into public/, the source snapshot cache, or any manifest field. Only the env var NAME is stored.

env.description: Environment variable name holding the secret (e.g. 'PRODUCTS_API_TOKEN'). Read via process.env at build time.

header.description: Request header the secret is placed in.

query.description: Alternatively, place the secret as a query parameter with this name (for APIs requiring ?api_key=...).

prefix.description: String prepended to the secret value, e.g. 'Bearer '.

select.description: type=http|json: JSONPath-lite expression selecting the item array from the response, e.g. '$.data.items' or '$.results'. Subset syntax: '$' / '.key' / '.a.b.c' / '[n]' (full grammar in data-sources.md §JSONPath-lite). Omit if the response root is already the array.

file.description: type=json|csv: local file path relative to site root, e.g. 'data/changelog.json'. Mutually exclusive with `url`.

items.description: type=inline: literal item array embedded in the manifest.

from.description: type=derived: name of the source this one is computed from.

op.description: type=derived: transform applied to the `from` source. groupBy produces one item per distinct value of `field` (with `{ slug, value, count, items }`); filter keeps items matching `where`; etc.

field.description: type=derived: field the op operates on (e.g. groupBy field='tags').

cache.description: Build-time snapshot cache for remote sources (http/json/csv/rss). The response is snapshotted to .xiaoyi-ssg/.cache/sources/<key>.json so offline/CI-without-secret builds can be reproduced via fallback='cache'. Snapshots MUST NOT contain secrets.

key.description: Stable cache key. Defaults to a hash of the source definition (minus secrets) when omitted.

ttl.description: Seconds the snapshot is considered fresh; within TTL the adapter reuses the snapshot without re-fetching. 0 = always refetch.

fallback.description: Behavior when a remote fetch fails (network error, non-2xx, missing secret): 'cache' = use the last snapshot if present else empty; 'empty' = produce zero items and warn; 'fail' = abort the build with a non-zero exit.
